title: "Oil surge sharpens calls for Indonesia to shift away from fossil fuels"
date: "2026-04-01T03:26:43.000Z"

region: Indonesia

## Summary
Surging global oil prices are intensifying pressure on Indonesia to accelerate its transition away from fossil fuel dependency toward renewable energy alternatives.

## Key Points
• Oil price surge amplifying calls for Indonesia to reduce fossil fuel dependence
• Energy transition pressure building on coal-dependent economy
• Policy pivot risk for coal miners (ITMG, ADRO) as renewable push intensifies
• Geothermal (PGEO) positioned as potential beneficiary of fossil fuel shift

## Portfolio Impact
🔴 **ITMG / ADRO**: Fossil fuel transition calls intensifying. Long-term structural headwind for coal plays, though near-term export demand may persist.

🟢 **PGEO**: Geothermal positioning strengthens as Indonesia pushes 100 GW solar/renewable target. Pertamina's green pivot could favor PGEO narrative.

**Position Check**: ITMG at 28,100 avg — watch for coal export policy changes. PGEO (1,011 avg) may benefit from energy transition narrative shift.
